title: Agents: AI assistants with 75K+ integrations built in | One
full_text:
agents
20,000+
Developers
83,891
Skills
99.9%
Uptime
<100ms
p95 Latency
An AI that actually does things
Most AI products give you answers. One Agent takes action -- across every app you use, through a single conversation.
One Agent connects to the apps you already use -- Gmail, Calendar, Slack, HubSpot, Jira, QuickBooks, Stripe, and hundreds more -- and does real work inside them. Not summaries. Not suggestions. Actual actions.
Tell it to triage your inbox and draft replies. Ask it to find every open deal over $50K in your CRM and update the forecast. Have it create a Jira ticket from a Slack thread. It reads, writes, and acts across 83,891 tools without you switching tabs.
83,891 skills, one agent
Skills are pre-built actions your agent can take: send an email, search contacts, create an invoice, update a record, file a ticket. One ships with over 80,000 of them across hundreds of apps.
A founder uses their agent to pull weekly revenue from Stripe, post it to Slack, and update a Notion dashboard. A support team routes incoming Zendesk tickets, drafts responses, and escalates blockers to engineering in Linear. A sales rep asks their agent to find every contact at Acme Corp across HubSpot, Gmail, and LinkedIn -- and gets a single answer in seconds.
You control which skills are active. Enable what you need, disable what you don't. Your agent only does what you let it.
Talk to it anywhere
Your agent lives on WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, or the web. Same agent, same context, same connected apps -- wherever you are.
Start a conversation on your phone during your commute. Pick it up on desktop at work. Ask a follow-up on Slack that afternoon. Your agent remembers what you asked and what it did.
If you're building a product on One, your users get the same flexibility. Deploy agents to any channel from a single API -- no messaging integrations to build or maintain.
It works while you don't
Agents don't just wait for you to ask. They react to events in real time.
A new lead lands in HubSpot -- your agent researches the company, enriches the contact, and drafts an outreach email. A payment hits Stripe -- your agent sends the receipt and updates the books. A support ticket gets escalated in Zendesk -- your agent pings the on-call engineer in Slack with full context.
You define the triggers. One handles webhook subscriptions, delivery, and retries.
See everything it does
Every action your agent takes is logged: what it did, when, in which app, and what happened. A simple timeline shows you exactly what's going on. No black box.
For developers, that means structured logs, tool execution traces, and error surfacing before users report them. For organizations, it means compliance-ready audit trails -- exportable, searchable, and tamper-proof.
Built on an open standard
One Agent is built on MCP (Model Context Protocol), the open standard for connecting AI to tools and data. Your agent works with Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any MCP-compatible client.
That means no lock-in. Your integrations travel with you across AI tools. And if you're building on One, every agent you ship gets MCP compatibility for free -- your users can connect to any client in the ecosystem without extra work on your end.
One agent or 100,000
Use One as a personal assistant with all your apps connected. Or spin up agents programmatically -- one per user, one per workspace, one per department.
A solo founder gets one agent that handles email, CRM, and calendar. A SaaS company provisions an agent per customer with isolated connections and permissions. An enterprise deploys a fleet across sales, support, and ops -- each with the right skills and guardrails -- managed from a single admin console.
Scale from one to a million without changing your architecture.
Under the hood
The agent runtime is built in Rust for speed and reliability. Skill execution runs at under 100ms p95 latency. Webhooks, scheduling, and message routing all happen at the infrastructure layer so your agent logic stays simple.
One works with every major LLM -- OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Llama, and anything else you bring. You pay your AI provider directly. One never charges for tokens. Bring your own model, swap providers any time, no lock-in.
Every integration authenticates through One's managed auth layer. OAuth flows, token refresh, and credential storage are handled automatically. Your agent never touches raw secrets -- it just calls skills and One handles the rest.
Security
All credentials are encrypted at rest and in transit. Each tenant gets full credential isolation -- one user's connections are never accessible to another. Every agent action is audit-logged with structured traces you can export and search.
SOC 2 compliance is in progress. For enterprise deployments, One supports SSO, SCIM provisioning, and granular permission policies so you control exactly what each agent and user can do.
Launch it your way
Launch
Create an agent from the dashboard, define one
in a project folder, or provision it programmatically.
Configure from the browser
Create and deploy agents from a visual interface. Name it, pick skills, assign channels, and go live. No code required.
Structure your agent as files. CLAUDE.md for instructions, skills as markdown, channels as code. Deploy with a single command.
Use the SDK to create, configure, and deploy agents from your own application. Full control for platform builders.
Define in a project folder
Structure your agent as files. CLAUDE.md for instructions, skills as markdown, channels as code. Deploy with a single command.
Provision programmatically
Use the SDK to create, configure, and deploy agents from your own application. Full control for platform builders.
app.withone.ai / new agent
Name support-agent
Skills gmail, hubspot, slack +8
Channels Slack #support, WhatsApp
Connections 3 verified
Instructions Triage incoming requests...
● Deployed Live
support-agent
support-agent
CLAUDE.md instructions
one.config.ts config
skills
email-triage.md
crm-sync.md
channels
slack.ts
whatsapp.ts
✓ one agent deploy Live
deploy.ts
import { One } from "@withone/sdk"
const agent = await One . agents . create ( {
name : "support-agent" ,
skills : [ "gmail" , "hubspot" , "slack" ],
channels : [
{ type : "slack" , target : "#support" }
} )
await agent . deploy ()
// => { status: "live", url: "..." }
▶ Show code example CLI SDK
# Initialize a new agent
one agent init --name "support-bot" \
--skills gmail,slack,zendesk
# Deploy to production
one agent deploy --env production
Frequently asked questions
What can One Agent do? +
One Agent connects to 500+ apps and takes real actions inside them -- send emails, update CRM records, create tickets, process payments, post to Slack, and more. It has 80,000+ pre-built skills that work out of the box with managed authentication.
Which LLMs does One Agent work with? +
All of them. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Llama, and any other model you bring. You pay your AI provider directly -- One never charges for tokens. Swap models any time with no lock-in.
How much does One Agent cost? +
Free to start, no credit card required. Transaction-based pricing with a fixed fee per agent as you scale. Bring your own tokens -- you pay your AI provider directly. Enterprise plans include SSO, SCIM, dedicated support, and custom SLAs.
How do I deploy an agent? +
Three ways: use the dashboard for no-code setup, run 'one agent init' from the CLI, or call One.agents.create() from the SDK. Deploy to Slack, WhatsApp, Telegram, or the web from a single configuration.
Is One Agent secure? +
All credentials are encrypted at rest and in transit with full tenant isolation. Every action is audit-logged. SOC 2 compliance is in progress. Enterprise deployments get SSO, SCIM provisioning, and granular permission policies.
What is MCP and why does it matter? +
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the open standard for connecting AI to tools. One is built on MCP, so your agents work with Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any MCP-compatible client. No vendor lock-in -- your integrations travel with you.
Can I use One Agent with bolt.new? +
Yes. One is bolt.new's official integration partner. Build your app in Bolt, then connect it to 500+ platforms through One -- Stripe, Gmail, Slack, HubSpot, and more. No boilerplate, production-ready from day one.
How is One different from Composio or other integration tools? +
Most platforms give you API wrappers. One gives you full agent infrastructure: managed OAuth with automatic token refresh, multi-tenant credential isolation, webhook-based automation, native MCP support, and 80,000+ production-tested skills. Built for production agents, not prototypes.
Get started
Connect your apps and put your agent to work. Or read the docs if you're building agents for your users.
Transaction-based pricing with a fixed fee per agent. Bring your own tokens -- you pay your AI provider directly. Free to start.
Try One Agent Read the docs Talk to us
Trusted by
Official integration partner of bolt.new new
Build full-stack apps with Bolt and connect them to 498 integrations through One.
Works with 498 platforms
One Agent integrates with Gmail, Google Calendar, Slack, Microsoft Teams, HubSpot, Salesforce, Stripe, Shopify, QuickBooks, Jira, Linear, Asana, Notion, GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Zendesk, Intercom, Freshdesk, Twilio, SendGrid, Mailchimp, Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, Box, Airtable, Monday.com, ClickUp, Trello, Zoom, Google Meet, Discord, WhatsApp, Telegram, PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Snowflake, BigQuery, AWS S3, Firebase, Supabase, Figma, Canva, LinkedIn, Twitter, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube, TikTok, Calendly, DocuSign, PandaDoc, Gusto, Rippling, Workday, NetSuite, Xero, Square, PayPal, Brex, Plaid, Segment, Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics, Datadog, PagerDuty, and hundreds more.
Each integration includes pre-built skills with managed OAuth, automatic token refresh, and production-tested reliability. One Agent is the official integration partner of bolt.new.

An AI that actually does things
Most AI products give you answers. One Agent takes action -- across every app you use, through a single conversation.
One Agent connects to the apps you already use -- Gmail, Calendar, Slack, HubSpot, Jira, QuickBooks, Stripe, and hundreds more -- and does real work inside them. Not summaries. Not suggestions. Actual actions.
Tell it to triage your inbox and draft replies. Ask it to find every open deal over $50K in your CRM and update the forecast. Have it create a Jira ticket from a Slack thread. It reads, writes, and acts across 83,891 tools without you switching tabs.
83,891 skills, one agent
Skills are pre-built actions your agent can take: send an email, search contacts, create an invoice, update a record, file a ticket. One ships with over 80,000 of them across hundreds of apps.
A founder uses their agent to pull weekly revenue from Stripe, post it to Slack, and update a Notion dashboard. A support team routes incoming Zendesk tickets, drafts responses, and escalates blockers to engineering in Linear. A sales rep asks their agent to find every contact at Acme Corp across HubSpot, Gmail, and LinkedIn -- and gets a single answer in seconds.
You control which skills are active. Enable what you need, disable what you don't. Your agent only does what you let it.
Talk to it anywhere
Your agent lives on WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, or the web. Same agent, same context, same connected apps -- wherever you are.
Start a conversation on your phone during your commute. Pick it up on desktop at work. Ask a follow-up on Slack that afternoon. Your agent remembers what you asked and what it did.
If you're building a product on One, your users get the same flexibility. Deploy agents to any channel from a single API -- no messaging integrations to build or maintain.
It works while you don't
Agents don't just wait for you to ask. They react to events in real time.
A new lead lands in HubSpot -- your agent researches the company, enriches the contact, and drafts an outreach email. A payment hits Stripe -- your agent sends the receipt and updates the books. A support ticket gets escalated in Zendesk -- your agent pings the on-call engineer in Slack with full context.
You define the triggers. One handles webhook subscriptions, delivery, and retries.
See everything it does
Every action your agent takes is logged: what it did, when, in which app, and what happened. A simple timeline shows you exactly what's going on. No black box.
For developers, that means structured logs, tool execution traces, and error surfacing before users report them. For organizations, it means compliance-ready audit trails -- exportable, searchable, and tamper-proof.
Built on an open standard
One Agent is built on MCP (Model Context Protocol), the open standard for connecting AI to tools and data. Your agent works with Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any MCP-compatible client.
That means no lock-in. Your integrations travel with you across AI tools. And if you're building on One, every agent you ship gets MCP compatibility for free -- your users can connect to any client in the ecosystem without extra work on your end.
One agent or 100,000
Use One as a personal assistant with all your apps connected. Or spin up agents programmatically -- one per user, one per workspace, one per department.
A solo founder gets one agent that handles email, CRM, and calendar. A SaaS company provisions an agent per customer with isolated connections and permissions. An enterprise deploys a fleet across sales, support, and ops -- each with the right skills and guardrails -- managed from a single admin console.
Scale from one to a million without changing your architecture.
Under the hood
The agent runtime is built in Rust for speed and reliability. Skill execution runs at under 100ms p95 latency. Webhooks, scheduling, and message routing all happen at the infrastructure layer so your agent logic stays simple.
One works with every major LLM -- OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Llama, and anything else you bring. You pay your AI provider directly. One never charges for tokens. Bring your own model, swap providers any time, no lock-in.
Every integration authenticates through One's managed auth layer. OAuth flows, token refresh, and credential storage are handled automatically. Your agent never touches raw secrets -- it just calls skills and One handles the rest.
Security
All credentials are encrypted at rest and in transit. Each tenant gets full credential isolation -- one user's connections are never accessible to another. Every agent action is audit-logged with structured traces you can export and search.
SOC 2 compliance is in progress. For enterprise deployments, One supports SSO, SCIM provisioning, and granular permission policies so you control exactly what each agent and user can do.

Use the SDK to create, configure, and deploy agents from your own application. Full control for platform builders.

deploy.ts
import { One } from "@withone/sdk"
const agent = await One . agents . create ( {
name : "support-agent" ,
skills : [ "gmail" , "hubspot" , "slack" ],
channels : [
{ type : "slack" , target : "#support" }
} )
await agent . deploy ()
// => { status: "live", url: "..." }

Frequently asked questions
What can One Agent do? +
One Agent connects to 500+ apps and takes real actions inside them -- send emails, update CRM records, create tickets, process payments, post to Slack, and more. It has 80,000+ pre-built skills that work out of the box with managed authentication.
Which LLMs does One Agent work with? +
All of them. OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Mistral, Llama, and any other model you bring. You pay your AI provider directly -- One never charges for tokens. Swap models any time with no lock-in.
How much does One Agent cost? +
Free to start, no credit card required. Transaction-based pricing with a fixed fee per agent as you scale. Bring your own tokens -- you pay your AI provider directly. Enterprise plans include SSO, SCIM, dedicated support, and custom SLAs.
How do I deploy an agent? +
Three ways: use the dashboard for no-code setup, run 'one agent init' from the CLI, or call One.agents.create() from the SDK. Deploy to Slack, WhatsApp, Telegram, or the web from a single configuration.
Is One Agent secure? +
All credentials are encrypted at rest and in transit with full tenant isolation. Every action is audit-logged. SOC 2 compliance is in progress. Enterprise deployments get SSO, SCIM provisioning, and granular permission policies.
What is MCP and why does it matter? +
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the open standard for connecting AI to tools. One is built on MCP, so your agents work with Cursor, Claude Desktop, and any MCP-compatible client. No vendor lock-in -- your integrations travel with you.
Can I use One Agent with bolt.new? +
Yes. One is bolt.new's official integration partner. Build your app in Bolt, then connect it to 500+ platforms through One -- Stripe, Gmail, Slack, HubSpot, and more. No boilerplate, production-ready from day one.
How is One different from Composio or other integration tools? +
Most platforms give you API wrappers. One gives you full agent infrastructure: managed OAuth with automatic token refresh, multi-tenant credential isolation, webhook-based automation, native MCP support, and 80,000+ production-tested skills. Built for production agents, not prototypes.
